ai-w9-e1.answer: Paste SecurityConfig. Ask what's missing: CSRF, CORS, permitAll too broad. Fix one issue.
ai-w9-e1.realWorld: /admin/** permitAll by mistake — lock to ROLE_ADMIN.

ai-w9-e2.answer: Ask security review of your API: auth on all routes?, SQL injection?, mass assignment?
ai-w9-e2.realWorld: Binding entity directly from request → DTO + validation instead.

ai-w9-e3.answer: Ask JWT flow: login → token → Authorization header → filter validates → SecurityContext.
ai-w9-e3.realWorld: Once you can draw the flow, Spring Security config makes sense.

ai-w9-e4.answer: Compare JWT vs server sessions: stateless vs stateful, logout, scale. Pick for your app type.
ai-w9-e4.realWorld: SPA + mobile often JWT; traditional web app may use session cookies.

ai-w9-e5.answer: Generate password rules: length, upper, digit, special. Implement in validator + test.

ai-w9-e6.answer: Review login endpoint: rate limit?, generic error messages?, BCrypt?
ai-w9-e6.realWorld: 'Invalid credentials' not 'User not found' — prevents user enumeration.

ai-w9-e7.answer: BCrypt hashes passwords one-way with salt. Never store plain text. Spring: BCryptPasswordEncoder.
ai-w9-e7.realWorld: passwordEncoder.encode(raw) on register; matches() on login.

ai-w9-e8.answer: Secure REST checklist: HTTPS, auth, validation, rate limit, CORS whitelist, no secrets in repo.
ai-w9-e8.realWorld: Score your project; fix top gap before portfolio demo.

ai-w9-e9.answer: Ask for auth flow diagram: register, login, refresh, protected call. Draw and label components.
ai-w9-e9.realWorld: Diagram in README shows security thinking to interviewers.

ai-w9-e10.answer: Common mistakes: JWT in localStorage XSS risk, long-lived tokens, no HTTPS, default admin password.
ai-w9-e10.realWorld: httpOnly cookie for token — discuss trade-offs with AI, decide consciously.
